title: carex acuta subsp. rufa
intro: Carex acuta subsp. rufa, commonly known as red-tipped sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Carex acuta subsp. rufa are believed to include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, and it is also sometimes used as an ingredient in salves and creams for topical application.
